title: State Transitions: Regular vs. Absorbing Markov Chains
description: A visual comparison of state transitions in a Regular Markov Chain, which converges to a stationary distribution, and a chain with Absorbing States, which eventually get trapped.
elements: Regular Markov Chain States (A, B, C)
relationships: All states in the regular chain communicate; arrows show probabilistic transitions between all states, eventually converging to π.
Arrows point into the absorbing state (Z) but none point out; arrows from transient states (X, Y) show possible paths into Z.
